message:
chore: add phage.db.gz to .gitignore to prevent future issues

CRITICAL: The build process runs VACUUM/REINDEX on phage.db which
changes its content hash. If phage.db.gz is committed from a
pre-optimized version, it will cause "Database hash mismatch" errors
that break the entire site.

Adding this to .gitignore ensures:
1. Nobody can accidentally commit a mismatched .db.gz
2. The app falls back to loading .db directly (which always works)
3. Future deployments are protected from this class of error

The .db.gz optimization should only be done if the build process
is updated to generate it AFTER running VACUUM/REINDEX.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -28,6 +28,8 @@ phage_illustrations/
 # Generated database (root level only, web public db is tracked for deployment)
 /phage.db
 !packages/web/public/phage.db
+# NEVER commit .db.gz - build process modifies DB, causing hash mismatches
+packages/web/public/phage.db.gz
 
 # Logs and coverage
 npm-debug.log*
